tool_args.label: Revenue Variance Driver Explanation
tool_args.raw_text: Total revenue of $618.3 million was $55.7 million lower than the Mid-Year Forecast (Forecast). This results from lower Capital Reimbursements partially offset by higher farebox revenue.

payload.claim_text: Total revenue of $618.3 million was $55.7 million lower than the Mid-Year Forecast. This results from lower Capital Reimbursements partially offset by higher farebox revenue.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad total revenue
payload.description: Explanation of total revenue variance vs Mid-Year Forecast

tool_args.label: Expense Variance Driver Explanation
tool_args.raw_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $1,498.6 million were $105.5 million or 6.6% favorable to the Forecast. The primary drivers of this favorable variance are the timing of expenses for material usage, lower professional service contracts and health and welfare costs, and vacant positions.

payload.claim_text: Total expenses before non-cash liability adjustments of $1,498.6 million were $105.5 million or 6.6% favorable to the Forecast. The primary drivers of this favorable variance are the timing of expenses for material usage, lower professional service contracts and health and welfare costs, and vacant positions.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad total expenses
payload.description: Explanation of expense variance vs Mid-Year Forecast

tool_args.label: Farebox Revenue Variance Driver
tool_args.raw_text: Farebox Revenues were $31.4 million favorable to the Forecast due to increased volumes as well as a shift in ridership from commutation to non-commutation, which generates a higher average yield.

payload.claim_text: Farebox Revenues were $31.4 million favorable to the Forecast due to increased volumes as well as a shift in ridership from commutation to non-commutation, which generates a higher average yield.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad farebox revenue
payload.description: Explanation of farebox revenue variance

tool_args.label: Other Operating Revenue Variance Driver
tool_args.raw_text: Other Operating Revenues were $0.8 million unfavorable to the Forecast due to lower Grand Central Terminal (GCT) retail revenue resulting from minimum rent adjustments as well as lower parking and stations rent partially offset by higher advertising revenue.

payload.claim_text: Other Operating Revenues were $0.8 million unfavorable to the Forecast due to lower Grand Central Terminal (GCT) retail revenue resulting from minimum rent adjustments as well as lower parking and stations rent partially offset by higher advertising revenue.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad other operating revenue
payload.description: Explanation of other operating revenue variance

tool_args.label: Preliminary Results Caveat
tool_args.raw_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are preliminary. - Actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.

payload.gap_text: Results are based on the preliminary close of the general ledger and are preliminary. Actuals do not include post-close adjustments, which will be captured in the subsequent month's YTD results.
payload.description: Financial results are preliminary and subject to post-close adjustments

tool_args.label: Labor Expense Line Items Variance Explanation
tool_args.raw_text: Labor Expenses: $37.6 million unfavorable to the Forecast. Payroll expenses were $10.4 million favorable to the Forecast primarily due to lower train and engine contractual payments. Overtime expenses were $19.0 million unfavorable to the Forecast primarily due to coverage required for must fill open positions. Health & Welfare expenses were $5.0 million favorable to the Forecast due to lower than forecasted rates partially offset by higher labor costs. ... Reimbursable Overhead expenses were $17.2 million unfavorable to the Forecast primarily due to scheduling and timing changes in capital project expenditures.

payload.claim_text: Payroll expenses were $10.4 million favorable to the Forecast primarily due to lower train and engine contractual payments. Overtime expenses were $19.0 million unfavorable primarily due to coverage required for must fill open positions. Health & Welfare expenses were $5.0 million favorable due to lower than forecasted rates partially offset by higher labor costs. Pension expenses were $10.3 million unfavorable due to higher rates and labor costs. Reimbursable Overhead expenses were $17.2 million unfavorable primarily due to scheduling and timing changes in capital project expenditures.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad labor expenses
payload.description: Breakdown of labor expense variances

tool_args.label: Non-Labor Expense Line Items Variance Explanation
tool_args.raw_text: Electric Power expenses were $6.7 million favorable to the Forecast due to lower usage. Fuel expenses were $2.3 million favorable to the Forecast due to lower usage. Maintenance and Other Operating Contracts were $7.9 million favorable to the Forecast primarily due to the timing of costs associated with the BL-20 locomotive overhaul

payload.claim_text: Electric Power expenses were $6.7 million favorable due to lower usage. Fuel expenses were $2.3 million favorable due to lower usage. Maintenance and Other Operating Contracts were $7.9 million favorable primarily due to timing of costs associated with the BL-20 locomotive overhaul as well as other miscellaneous maintenance and operating contracts.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad non-labor expenses
payload.description: Breakdown of non-labor expense variances

tool_args.label: Additional Non-Labor Expense Variances
tool_args.raw_text: Professional Service Contracts were $4.8 million favorable to the Forecast primarily due to a prior year true-up for lower BSC/IT allocations as well as lower than anticipated consulting and engineering services. Materials and Supplies were $33.0 million favorable to the Forecast primarily due to the timing of rolling stock maintenance events and rolling stock material usage

payload.claim_text: Professional Service Contracts were $4.8 million favorable primarily due to a prior year true-up for lower BSC/IT allocations as well as lower than anticipated consulting and engineering services. Materials and Supplies were $33.0 million favorable primarily due to the timing of rolling stock maintenance events and rolling stock material usage partially offset by higher material adjustments.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad non-labor expenses
payload.description: Additional non-labor expense variances including Professional Service Contracts and Materials

tool_args.label: Other Business Expenses Variance
tool_args.raw_text: Other Business Expenses were $2.6 million favorable to the Forecast primarily due to expense recoveries for the operation and maintenance of M8 rail cars used for Shore Line East Service, lower credit card processing fees, higher Amtrak recoveries and lower miscellaneous expenses partially offset by higher subsidy payments to New Jersey Transit resulting from inflationary adjustments.

payload.claim_text: Other Business Expenses were $2.6 million favorable primarily due to expense recoveries for the operation and maintenance of M8 rail cars used for Shore Line East Service, lower credit card processing fees, higher Amtrak recoveries and lower miscellaneous expenses partially offset by higher subsidy payments to New Jersey Transit resulting from inflationary adjustments.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad other business expenses
payload.description: Other business expenses variance explanation

tool_args.label: November Forecast vs Actual Variance Explanation
tool_args.raw_text: Total Revenue $11.3 2.7 Higher farebox revenue as a result of increased ridership and increased advertising revenue partially offset by lower GCT retail and commuter parking revenue. Total Expenses ($16.6) (1.1) Primarily reflects lower overhead recoveries and higher overtime expense partially offset by lower non-cash liability adjustments, regular pay, and professional services costs.

payload.claim_text: Total Revenue $11.3M favorable (2.7%) due to higher farebox revenue from increased ridership and increased advertising revenue partially offset by lower GCT retail and commuter parking revenue. Total Expenses ($16.6M) unfavorable (1.1%) primarily due to lower overhead recoveries and higher overtime expense partially offset by lower non-cash liability adjustments, regular pay, and professional services costs.
payload.subject: Metro-North Railroad November Forecast vs Actual
payload.description: Explanation of variances between November Forecast and Actual results for November 2022 Year-to-Date
